title: Server certificates should be verified during SSL/TLS connections
description: This rule checks if SSL/TLS connections are performed without verifying the server's certificate.
impact: Potential data leaks or man-in-the-middle attacks
recommendation: Always verify the server's certificate during SSL/TLS connections.

noncompliant.code:
import ssl
context = ssl.create_default_context()
context.check_hostname = False
socket = context.wrap_socket(socket, server_hostname=None)
socket.connect(('hostname', 443))

compliant.code:
import ssl
context = ssl.create_default_context()
socket = context.wrap_socket(socket, server_hostname='hostname')
socket.connect(('hostname', 443))

why_is_this_an_issue: Unverified server certificates can lead to data leaks or man-in-the-middle attacks.
how_to_fix: Always verify the server's certificate during SSL/TLS connections.

metadata.title: Server certificates should be verified during SSL/TLS connections
